# Release review instructions

- Releases are triggered when release-plz pushes a `v<version>` tag after the release PR is merged into `main`. There is no manual tagging step.
- `release.yml` is triggered by tag pushes matching `v*.*.*` and `*.*.*`. The globs match the `v<version>` tags release-plz produces and preserve the existing cargo-dist trigger shape. It is autogenerated by cargo-dist; regenerate it with `dist generate --ci github` after changing `dist-workspace.toml`, then reapply the Rust toolchain update and OIDC publish job if cargo-dist drops them.
- The workflow runs five cargo-dist jobs (`plan`, `build-local-artifacts`, `build-global-artifacts`, `host`, `announce`) followed by a `publish` job.
- The `publish` job depends on `host` and publishes to crates.io using OIDC trusted publishing. It requires `id-token: write` for the OIDC token and `contents: read` because the job declares scoped permissions and uses `actions/checkout`. It uses `rust-lang/crates-io-auth-action` to exchange the GitHub OIDC token for a short-lived crates.io token. No `CARGO_REGISTRY_TOKEN` secret is stored.
- crates.io Trusted Publisher must be configured in the crate settings once, with owner `major`, repo `volumeleaders-rs`, workflow file `release.yml`, and package `rusty-volumeleaders`.
- Changelog generation is handled by release-plz (via `cliff.toml`) when it opens the release PR. Do not regenerate `CHANGELOG.md` by hand on `main`.
- The release commit produced by release-plz follows the pattern `chore: release v<version>`. The tag is an unsigned annotated tag pushed via the GitHub API using `RELEASE_PLZ_TOKEN`. Tag signing was intentionally dropped when adopting release-plz.
- Do not add `cargo publish` steps or `git_release_enable = true` in `release-plz.toml`; cargo-dist owns crates.io publication and the GitHub Release.
